title: 10 Reconstructing Lost CRA Remittance Voucher Bundles
body_text:
10	Reconstructing Lost CRA Remittance Voucher Bundles
10.1	Overview of Reconstructing Lost CRA Remittance Voucher Bundles
Remittance vouchers that canât be processed in Business Taxes must be sent to CRA by mail or courier. See Chapter 9, Processing Remittance Vouchers Manually, for more information.
If the draft isnât cashed after a month or if CRA advises that the funds are missing, the bundle may be lost in the mail.
10.2	Resubmitting Lost Remittance Voucher Bundles
Proceed as follows if a remittance voucher bundle is lost in the mail:
Place a stop-payment on the draft sent with the original remittance voucher bundle.
Obtain photocopies of the remittance vouchers.
Create a tape listing of the copied remittance vouchers. Ensure that each remittance voucher is included on the tape listing. Keep the order of the remittance vouchers in the same sequence as the tape listing.
Re-issue a draft for the total of the bundle and address it to the Receiver General.
Write a letter to CRA on your financial institutionâs letterhead. Advise that the reconstructed bundle is being sent because the original bundle was lost in the mail. Include a contact name and phone number in the letter.
Make copies of the remittance voucher copies, tape listing, draft, and the letter.
Send the bundle of remittance voucher photocopies and the replacement draft via mail or courier to CRA:
Canada Revenue Agency
875 Heron Road
Ottawa, Ontario K1A 1B1
When CRA receives the bundle, it will use the date recorded on the remittance voucher photocopies as the date paid and your customer wonât incur a penalty or interest. If applicable, the penalty or interest will be reversed.
END
